About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4047G
Zegona Communications PLC
01 June 2026
1 June 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
Total Voting Rights
Zegona hereby notifies that its capital as of 31 May 2026 comprised 226,163,802 ordinary shares of 1 pence each. Zegona holds no shares in treasury and the total voting rights in Zegona as of 31 May 2026 were 226,163,802.
The above figure of 226,163,802 should be used by shareholders (and others with notification obligations) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, Zegona under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona Ltd. provides technology‑enabled telecom infrastructure solutions, mainly focusing on fibre‑optic and 5G network deployments for mobile operators and enterprises.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the heart of the global shift to high‑speed connectivity. As operators upgrade to 5G and backhaul capacity, Zegona’s fibre and node equipment become essential components in a rapidly expanding market that is still worth billions of pounds.
company_30s.current_state: Zegona is in a cash‑burn phase, having recently sold a portion of its own shares and secured voting rights to keep control. The business is still building its revenue base, but the latest FTSE Global Equity Index Series inclusion signals that it has met certain liquidity and reporting standards.

rns_narrative: The company has just completed a share‑repurchase, which is a sign that it is looking to manage its capital structure and possibly signal confidence in its own valuation.
A vote on total voting rights and a disclosure of holdings suggest that management is consolidating control, a move that can be interpreted as a stabilisation effort.
The FTSE Global Equity Index Series listing is a milestone that may improve visibility to institutional investors and provide a new source of capital.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around dividends and share‑repurchase
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter is driven by a few high‑profile investors talking about the upcoming dividend and the potential upside from a share buyback.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices focus on the lack of real revenue and the risk that the company could be over‑valued for a cash‑burn business.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation is slowly warming from neutral to mildly bullish as the dividend news surfaces, but the overall chatter remains sparse.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public search activity. This silence is a classic contrarian cue that the market hasn’t yet priced in any forthcoming catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: A dividend payout on the 9th of January – the day after the ex‑dividend date – could trigger a short‑term rally as investors re‑buy shares.
Any announcement of new 5G contracts or fibre projects would provide a fundamental driver for a longer‑term rebound.
A sudden spike in trading volume (double the daily average) would signal that institutional capital is beginning to re‑enter the stock.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 10–15% volume surge that coincides with a price break above the 20‑day moving average. Also monitor the share‑repurchase schedule – if the company buys back shares at a price that is 20% above the current level, it will act as a price floor.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation‑to‑recovery cycle. With an APEX score above 8, the market is still in a compression phase; a single catalyst could unlock a significant mean‑reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is 86% below its all‑time high – a deep crash that historically produces the biggest rebounds. The recent dividend and share‑repurchase are classic signals that the firm is ready to recover. If the price climbs to the midpoint of its pre‑crash range, the upside is roughly 2× the current level. The asymmetry is favourable because the downside is capped at zero – the stock can only fall further if the business fails.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure new contracts or if the dividend is perceived as a one‑off event, the stock may stay depressed. A worst‑case scenario is a further decline to zero if the business cannot generate sustainable cash flow.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 2× current price. Potential downside: 100% (price could fall to zero). Risk‑reward ratio: 2:1 – a classic high‑reward, high‑risk contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 20‑day moving average and volume is 1.5× the average. Allocate 10–15% of the trading capital per position and set a stop 5% below the entry to limit loss to a single‑digit percentage of the stake.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until a 10–15% volume spike occurs or until the company announces a new 5G contract. Watch the price for a bounce to the 20‑day moving average; that will be the trigger for a full entry.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks have been identified, so the play remains bullish. If a bankruptcy filing or delisting notice appears, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Zegona is a deep‑crashed telecom infrastructure firm that has just begun to show signs of stabilization; a dividend payout or new contract announcement could spark a two‑fold recovery, making it a high‑reward, high‑risk contrarian opportunity.
